summary: Closed approved planning lanes, archived completed plans, and established a concrete end-of-day Samwise self-improvement system with diary logging.

Added Samwise daily closeout essential, intent, directive, and session
Created samwise-diary JSONL + schema and seeded first entry
whatLearned: Action-only TODO queues reduce planning noise and decision fatigue
A stable closeout ritual improves restart speed and partner continuity
Self-improvement logs are more useful when they include concrete next-step goals
improvements: Run Samwise closeout session at end of each workday before final signoff
Keep partner-context updates synchronized with diary entries when partner strategy changes
Review backlog items weekly so medium-priority maintenance work does not stagnate
nextGoals: Execute PSG-002 session lifecycle states across session inventory
Execute PSG-003 recurring closeout checklist integration
Continue proposal-first git-history gardening passes

summary: Completed a governance hardening cycle for gardening: codified worktree-scoped commit authorization, introduced a reusable Gardening v2 checklist, clarified self-gardening approval parity, and named the pack gardener persona Rosie for clearer collaboration continuity.
whatShipped: Enforced commit authorization policy where standing auto-commit authority is valid only for AJ-approved dedicated non-main worktrees
Wired Gardening v2 checklist into pack gardener standards, kit, intent, and directive
Added explicit self-gardening guardrails so Rosie follows the same analysis-first and approval-gated flow
Named pack gardener persona as Rosie while preserving stable persona ID `pack-gardener`
Verified Personakit validation and gardening-log integrity after updates
whatLearned: Governance changes are safer when policy text is synchronized across persona, essentials, directives, intents, and logs in one pass
Self-gardening needs explicit policy text to prevent implicit exceptions
Human-readable persona names improve collaboration while stable IDs preserve tool compatibility
On `main`, commit mode remains explicit per-commit AJ approval because no worktree auto-commit scope is active
improvements: Add a dedicated targeted-gardening session template so focusArea and changedArtifact scoping is first-class in workflow prompts
Add a lightweight closeout validator for `samwise-diary.jsonl` schema checks
Document a standard handoff snippet for starting a new thread with current priorities and known open backlog IDs
nextGoals: Start the next thread with Samwise using the latest closeout context and active open backlog items
Address open backlog item PSG-002 (session lifecycle states)
Address open backlog item PSG-003 (recurring closeout checklist integration)
Run Rosie in targeted mode on a single pack element to validate focused-gardening ergonomics

whatLearned: Dual MCP server routing removes scope ambiguity between main and dedicated lane worktrees
Keeping lane-specific rules out default kits prevents policy bleed into unrelated sessions
Small hardening commits are effective for converting implied constraints into explicit local guardrails
On `rosies-garden`, standing AJ-approved auto-commit scope remains valid for this dedicated non-main worktree
improvements: Add a startup MCP scope smoke check (`catalog/index` root verification) to lane workflows
Add a closeout checklist line for commit hashes queued for mainline integration
Consider a recurring reminder for daily Samwise closeout in this worktree
nextGoals: Integrate today's Rosie lane commits into `main` in the main worktree
Re-sync `rosies-garden` with updated `main` baseline after integration

summary: Finished today’s governance and continuity lane by closing session lifecycle-state documentation (PSG-002), adopting a recurring closeout checklist (PSG-003), and recording git-history analysis pass #4 with no pending cleanup proposals.
whatShipped: Added session lifecycle-state convention doc and labeled all current sessions in the directory (`active`/`candidate`/`deprecated`)
Closed backlog item PSG-002 and updated TODO/maintenance logs to reflect completion
Added a dedicated Development closeout checklist and linked it from development/docs indexes

whatLearned: Lifecycle-state visibility reduces ambiguity when choosing sessions under time pressure
A single closeout checklist lowers the chance that maintenance and logging steps are skipped during transitions
Analysis-only git-history passes are useful even when no edits are made because they preserve decision traceability
Keeping TODO focused on truly open work improves restart clarity for the next session
improvements: Add a lightweight docs reminder to review and refresh `Last Reviewed` fields during closeout passes
Add an optional checklist line to capture whether any follow-up commit approval is needed before ending the day
Keep using analysis-only first for gardening changes, then move to approved execution only when AJ confirms
nextGoals: Run the next bounded Taskboard pilot loop and capture usage feedback against the parity-ready baseline

summary: Completed the Rosie gardening program for this checkpoint: landed phase 1-4 tooling (observability, scoring, coverage auditing, policy-conflict detection, and safety preflight), ran a critical hardening pass, and committed the full lane update.
whatShipped: Added deterministic gardening telemetry streams and schemas for health snapshots, scored recommendations, feedback outcomes, coverage audits, policy conflicts, and safety preflight
Wired new gardening contracts into Rosie/Samwise upkeep directives, intents, kits, and standards for routine enforcement
Expanded `Scripts/check-gardening-logs.sh` to validate score math, ranking sets, reference integrity, policy invariants, and safety-preflight invariants
Addressed critical review findings by adding `rankingSetId`, aligning `GREC-*` ID contract semantics, and replacing brittle phrase checks with structural policy checks
Committed all completed changes as `feat(gardening): complete phase 1-4 tooling and detectors` (`8f4a845`)
whatLearned: Recommendation ranking needs explicit ranking-set boundaries to avoid false failures when multiple passes happen on the same date
ID-contract wording must align with validator behavior or maintenance loops become fragile
Structural policy assertions are more resilient than free-text substring checks for long-term guardrail enforcement
Commit authorization mode remained explicit and valid under AJ-approved standing auto-commit scope for this dedicated non-main worktree (`rosies-garden`)
improvements: Add a dedicated script test fixture with two same-day ranking sets to prevent regression in ranking validation logic
Add a small closeout verifier script for `samwise-diary.jsonl` schema checks
Add a pre-integration checklist line for "lane commit hash to replay onto main" in closeout routine
nextGoals: Replay lane commit `8f4a845` onto `main` in the main worktree per approved lane/main flow
Sync updated `main` back into `rosies-garden` and record residual risks/verification
Continue ongoing gardening-tooling operations queue with routine detector snapshots on future non-trivial passes

summary: Hardened Samwise's reusable squad-planning workflow so a new product brief can be staffed, logged, remediated for missing roles, and handed forward with a durable restart trail, then committed the workflow stack on `main`.
whatShipped: Added reusable squad-planning artifacts for planning, logging, remediation, and orchestration across workspaces
Created durable planning-report and JSONL contracts plus `Scripts/check-squad-planning-logs.sh`
Added `samwise-squad-planning-remediation` so execution-critical missing roles route through reverse-interview and hiring discipline before execution
Updated Samwise defaults to include `samwise-orchestration-core` and recorded the bootstrap planning review/report
Committed the workflow stack as `feat(personakit): harden samwise squad planning loops` (`79c63bc`)
whatLearned: A planning workflow is much more trustworthy once it produces both a human report and a machine-readable handoff record
Missing-role handling needs its own named loop; otherwise reverse-interview expectations stay advisory instead of operational
For cross-thread continuity, the most valuable handoff detail is the first concrete re-entry action rather than a broad summary
On `main`, commit authority remains per-commit AJ approval, so each follow-up commit still needs explicit confirmation
improvements: Add a schema validator for `samwise-diary.jsonl` so closeout continuity has the same enforcement as hiring and squad-planning logs
Refresh the stale gardening pack-coverage snapshot so repo-wide maintenance telemetry matches current artifact counts
Dogfood `samwise-squad-planning` on a real product brief and capture whether any additional stop gates or output fields are still missing
nextGoals: Start the next Samwise thread from `Docs/Orbit/Planning/Orbit-Execution-Plan.md` and run the first real `samwise-squad-planning` pass against the Orbit brief
If planning identifies execution-critical uncovered roles, route immediately to `samwise-squad-planning-remediation` before any implementation handoff
After the first real planning pass, refresh gardening coverage snapshots so `Scripts/check-gardening-logs.sh` returns to green

summary: Closed the first squad-pattern evaluation checkpoint by recording the generated-workstream-docs closeout, reviewing the delegated-grounding planning-stack hardening as a second trial, and deciding that worktree squads are situational rather than default.
whatShipped: Recorded a formal Trial 1 worktree-squad retrospective for the generated workstream-docs pipeline.
Recorded a formal Trial 2 worktree-squad retrospective for delegated-agent grounding and handoff refinement.
Wrote a comparison memo scoring both trials and recommending `situational` rather than `standard` or `not default`.
Updated partner continuity guidance so future squad use is framed as a boundary-heavy and review-sensitive tool instead of a default for all medium work.
whatLearned: Squads create the most value when the main risk is boundary drift, source-of-truth ambiguity, or multi-role review discipline.
Medium complexity alone does not guarantee useful parallelism; highly coupled implementation paths still behave sequentially.
Process-contract hardening benefited more from explicit squad roles than the generated-doc feature did, even though both used the same basic structure.
improvements: Start each future squad trial with an explicit rubric for scope control, role clarity, gate value, parallelism, overhead, and reusability.
Decide upfront whether a candidate assignment is boundary-heavy enough to justify squad overhead before staffing roles.
Keep actual-participant evidence explicit in retrospectives so process claims stay honest.
nextGoals: Apply the situational squad rule to the next medium-complexity assignment before staffing it.
If the duplicate-ID guarantee assignment proceeds, decide whether it truly needs squad gates or should run as a simpler lane.
Revisit the squad decision only after a future trial materially challenges the current situational verdict.

summary: Closed the Samwise closeout-wrapper hardening checkpoint by turning the new script into a safer default path for continuity logging, rollback, and verification.
whatShipped: Added `./Scripts/samwise-closeout.sh` as a wrapped closeout command that appends partner-context and Samwise diary records, then runs the existing projection and verification commands.
Extended `./Scripts/check-operational-records.sh` so Samwise diary entries are validated alongside the other canonical operational ledgers.

Hardened the wrapper after red-pen review so it validates ledger monotonicity before writing, computes next IDs safely, persists rollback metadata before append, and restores the partner-context markdown snapshot on rollback.
Documented the wrapped closeout path in the development closeout checklist and logs README so future checkpoint use is discoverable.
whatLearned: For append-only operational logs, helper scripts need rollback boundaries that are explicit enough to survive infrastructure failures, not just happy-path validation.
Generated markdown companions are part of the closeout state, so rollback planning has to account for them instead of treating JSONL as the only mutable surface.
The Samwise review posture worked well here because the script stayed small while the trust guarantees became much clearer.
improvements: If this workflow grows again, consider moving the append plus projection logic behind a first-class PersonaKit CLI command so one tool owns the transaction boundary.
Add a focused failure-mode test or harness for the closeout wrapper so rollback behavior can be exercised more directly than shell dry-runs.
Keep future Samwise process helpers small and explicitly documented rather than relying on memory or scattered checklist prose.
nextGoals: Commit this bounded closeout-wrapper hardening slice with one conventional commit after the closeout finishes cleanly.
Use the wrapped closeout path at the next explicit Samwise checkpoint now that the rollback guarantees are clearer.
Revisit broader transaction semantics only if a future checkpoint exposes a real remaining failure mode.
